title: rubus myricae subsp. pervirescens
intro: Rubus myricae subsp. pervirescens, also known as the dwarf raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus myricae plant, a type of bramble native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and tannins, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus myricae subsp. pervirescens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
